title: Key details about iPhone 18 Pro leak online after hackers gain access to 630 GB data from Tata Electronics
snippet: Leaks from Tata Electronics reveal details about Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro, including component suppliers and internal documents after a ransomware attack. This breach, involving over 200,000 files, threatens Apple's supplier relationships and business strategy ahead of the September launch.

title: Samsung Galaxy A27 5G debuts in India with 120Hz AMOLED display, price starts at  ₹28,999
snippet: Samsung Galaxy A27 5G features a 6.7-inch Full HD+ Super AMOLED display, Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 chipset, 50MP triple rear camera, and 5,000mAh battery.&nbsp;

title: OnePlus N6 5G launched in India with 8,000mAh battery, Dimensity 6360 Apex processor: Check price and specs
snippet: The OnePlus N6 features a 6.75-inch HD display, MediaTek Dimensity 6360 Apex processor, 8,000mAh battery, and military-grade durability.&nbsp;

title: WhatsApp to let users reserve usernames from next week: Here's how it works
snippet: WhatsApp introduces a username system to eliminate the need to share your phone number. The company will allow users to select their usernames starting next week.&nbsp;&nbsp;

title: WhatsApp to let users chat without sharing phone numbers: Username feature coming soon
snippet: WhatsApp, which says it has more than 3 billion users globally, has until now allowed users to be contacted by anyone who has their phone number.

title: Will iPhone 17 Pro Max cost nearly  ₹2 lakh in India? Leak suggests so
snippet: The iPhone 17 Pro, currently priced at  ₹1,34,900, could reportedly retail between  ₹1,54,900 and  ₹1,69,900. The iPhone 17 Pro Max may be priced between  ₹1,84,900 and  ₹1,99,900, compared with its current price of  ₹1,49,900.

title: Samsung Galaxy M47 5G launched in India with 6,000mAh battery, 50MP OIS camera
snippet: Samsung has launched the Galaxy M47 5G in India with a price of  ₹22,999. The phone features a Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 chipset, 6.7-inch AMOLED display, 6,000mAh battery, and a 50MP camera.&nbsp;

title: Apple prepares smart glasses to take on Meta, launch tipped for next year
snippet: Apple is eyeing the smart glasses market, potentially releasing its first model next year. Apple could be looking to leverage its strong ecosystem to bring in buyers, a report claims

title: iPhone 18 Pro launch date, expected price, processor and camera upgrades
snippet: Leaks suggest Apple's iPhone 18 Pro lineup may launch on September 8 or 9. The phone is expected to see a price of around $1,200-$1,300

title: iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max could see up to $200 price hike, says report
snippet: Apple could raise the prices for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models by up to $200. The iPhone 18 Pro could be priced at $1,200-$1,300, if the new report is to be believed

title: South Korean trade watchdog alleges Google abused its position in Android app store
snippet: South Korea's antitrust regulator alleged on Wednesday that ​Alphabet's Google abused its ​dominant position in the Android app marketplace to ​hinder competition and will recommend corrective measures and a financial penalty.

title: Fear and anger brew inside Meta amid AI frenzy
snippet: A frenzied push for artificial intelligence dominance comes with a different kind of cost for Meta, where massive layoffs, employee surveillance and departures have fueled reports of a heated internal climate. title: Meta loses bid to dismiss US states' claims that Facebook, Instagram addict children
snippet: In a decision late Monday, U.S. District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in Oakland, California, denied Meta's motion to dismiss claims based on deception, unfair practices and violations of the federal Children's Online Privacy Protection Act.

title: Microsoft to cut under 2.5% of workforce in latest layoffs: report
snippet: U.S. companies ‌have continued ⁠to trim ⁠headcount in recent months, with a fresh wave of layoffs across the ​technology, media and finance sectors as firms rein in costs, while investing heavily ​in AI infrastructure.

title: US govt lifts restrictions on powerful AI models, Anthropic says
snippet: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday. title: VC fundraising hits wall; WhatsApp’s new privacy headache
snippet: Indian VC firms are facing their hardest fundraising year in a decade as LPs tighten filters and demand cash returns. This and more in today's Morning Dispatch.

title: Orchestration layer is new battleground for Indian AI talent
snippet: ​​Recruiters told ET that hiring for standalone prompt engineering roles has plateaued as companies increasingly seek engineers who can build and orchestrate agentic or autonomous AI systems, signalling one of the fastest shifts yet in the country’s AI talent market.

title: UK investors sue Binance in London for £150 million
snippet: The claimants, some ‌of whom ⁠said ⁠they had lost tens of thousands of pounds, allege ​Binance entities knowingly sold investments such as leveraged products, ​which can amplify gains or losses, from late 2019 and promoted them in breach of ​the Financial Services and Markets ⁠Act.

title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.
snippet: It’s been hot in London this week. Really hot. A dangerous heat wave has hit Western Europe. Yesterday, the UK recorded its highest ever June temperature at 36.1 °C (about 97 °F). But as the weather app on my phone confirmed, it felt like 39 °C. It’s frightening that we are seeing such temperatures
